galerie_desc: An eye for the details others overlook — in code as well as in the cityscape. Where technical case studies meet urban observations.
baustellen_desc: Some projects are like old cathedrals: they are never truly finished. An honest look at my current ongoing projects, drafts, and R&D loops.
baustellen_wohin_desc: Vibe-oriented spot discovery platform. Search and filter by activities instead of stars.
baustellen_wohin_irony: Backend and CMS are ready, but switching from Expo to Flutter resets app development back to square one.
baustellen_quiz_desc: Modern political profiling and playful this-or-that tournament as a Wahl-O-Mat alternative.
baustellen_quiz_irony: Technically almost finished, but fine-tuning political party weightings feels like real coalition talks.
baustellen_party_desc: Jackbox-style party game for local multiplayer nights. Your own smartphone becomes the controller.
baustellen_party_irony: Right in the concept phase with a close friend.
expertise_card1_desc: Enterprise systems with Java & Spring Boot — from conception to deployment. Clean layers, clear responsibilities, scalable structures.
expertise_card2_desc: Complex domain logic for platforms like the 'Amazon for farmers'. Systems that process millions of transactions reliably.
expertise_card3_desc: Analyzing existing architectures, identifying bottlenecks, hardening systems. No quick fixes — structural improvement.
hero_subtitle: By day I build systems as a Software Architect that just work. After that I roam the city with my camera — or tinker on things nobody asked for.
kontakt_desc: Direct contact. No masks. No form that has to go through three departments. Write to me, and we'll talk about your project.
offgrid_intro: A brief glimpse into analog daily life away from screens and code structures — a conversation starter for everything unrelated to work.
offgrid_reading_desc: Creative focus and the craft of creation
offgrid_coffee_desc: Light roast, brewed in a Chemex
philosophie_title:
Code is architecture.
Not assembly line work.
philosophie_p1: For me, software is not the product of a factory, but the result of craftsmanship. Every architecture decision has consequences — like the statics of a building. I build systems that last. Not ones you throw away next year.
philosophie_p2: This stance is not a luxury, it is a necessity. Moving complex domains into clean structures doesn't save time during building — but it saves years in maintenance.
wert_fundiert_desc: Everything has weight and depth. No superficiality, no empty promises.
wert_charakterstark_desc: Independence is not a defect. Striking choices lead to unique results.
wert_substanziell_desc: Tactile, valuable, of lasting significance — like heavy wool fabric.
wert_nuanciert_desc: An eye for the details others overlook. In code as in the cityscape.
wert_autonom_desc: Independent solutions, far from the IT monoculture. No herd mentality.
wert_bestaendig_desc: Building systems that last. No throwaway architecture, no quick fixes.
stat_band_note: — the quick profile, without the sales pitch.
stat1_note: and none of them have bored me
stack_desc: Scroll through the stack — each card goes deeper into one side of my work.
stack_card1_desc: Enterprise architecture with Java & Spring Boot — clean layers, clear responsibilities, scalable structures.
stack_card2_desc: Street photography from Hamburg, London, and Hannover. High contrast, deliberate light and shadow.
stack_card3_desc: An honest look at projects that never quite finish — from the vibe-discovery tool to the party game.
stack_card4_desc: Principles, philosophy, and daily context away from screens — Chemex coffee, modal jazz, Seth Godin.
arbeit_head_lede: Enterprise architecture with Java & Spring Boot — clean layers, clear responsibilities, scalable structures. Plus the side projects that never quite finish.
fotografie_head_lede: Street photography from Hamburg, London and Hannover. High contrast, deliberate light and shadow.
ueber_head_lede: Principles, philosophy and the current context away from the screen — Chemex coffee, modal jazz, Seth Godin.
ueber_bio_p1: By day I build systems as a software architect — systems that just run. Twelve-plus years of it, across Hamburg, Hannover and London.
ueber_bio_p2: After that I am out in the city with a camera, or tinkering with things nobody asked for. Boring in production is a compliment; boring in my head it never was.
